children.name: Accordion
children.description: Accordions help users focus by enabling them to show and hide content.

children.name: Avatar
children.description: Avatars represent people, companies, or entities using an icon, image, or initials within a container.

children.name: Badge
children.description: Displays contextual information, like user roles, statuses, or categories.

children.name: Breadcrumbs
children.description: Breadcrumbs help users navigate complex websites. They show the path from the top to the current page, and let you easily backtrack to previous pages.

children.name: Button Groups
children.description: Used to group related actions or options together. Button groups allow users to make selections or trigger actions without navigating away.

children.name: Button Icons
children.description: Button icons are simple and compact, saving space in the Salesforce user interface. They're often used for actions that are easy to understand from the icon alone, like closing a window or editing an

children.name: Cards
children.description: A card is used to group similar types of information, such as recent records, upcoming tasks, or alerts.

children.name: Checkbox
children.description: Enables you to select and deselect individual items within a group of options or turn settings on and off.

children.name: Checkbox Button
children.description: Used for single selections as it switches between checked and unchecked states

children.name: Checkbox Toggle
children.description: Checkbox toggle enables you to switch settings between two binary states, such as enabling or disabling features.

children.name: Combobox
children.description: Commonly used for selecting from a large list of options.

children.name: Components
children.description: SLDS 2 includes a curated collection of reusable components that guide and inform users’ interactions within our products and apps. These components are designed to make it easy to create a consistent

children.name: Data Table
children.description: Data Tables are ideal for organizing data into a structured format, enabling users to view, manage, and interact with data quickly.

children.name: Datepicker
children.description: The datepicker is designed to help users select dates quickly and accurately from a calendar view.

children.name: Dual Listbox
children.description: Select multiple items from one list and move them to another, building a list of selected items in the right-hand list.

children.name: Dynamic Icons
children.description: Dynamic icons provide animated visual cues that indicate specific actions, states, or insights. This immediate visual feedback helps users understand the function and status of elements in the interfa

children.name: File Selector
children.description: Used to upload files for tasks such as attaching documents to records or sharing media.

children.name: Horizontal List
children.description: Horizontal list utility classes provide an easy way to create and manage horizontally aligned lists. These classes handle spacing, alignment, and responsive behavior for list items, ensuring a clean,

children.name: Icons
children.description: Icons are symbols used to represent features, functionality, or content.

children.name: Icons
children.description: Icons are symbols used to represent features, functionality, or content. Visit the icon page to see a library of all icons and the icon component for implementation details.

children.name: Input
children.description: Used for collecting user data such as names, emails, and numerical entries.

children.name: Menu
children.description: Use a menu to offer a list of actions a user can take on a page or a specific record.

children.name: Name Value List
children.description: Name-value list utility classes help create structured, easy-to-read lists where each item consists of a label (name) and corresponding data (value). These utility classes ensure consistent alignment,

children.name: Pills
children.description: Pills display compact labels for items, such as objects or selections, which a user can quickly add, view, or remove.

children.name: Popover
children.description: Popovers are non-modal dialogs that give the user contextual information and actions on canvas elements, and message users about errors and warnings.

children.name: Progress Bar
children.description: The Progress Bar is a visual indicator that shows the ongoing progress of a task, indicating that a user’s request is being processed.

children.name: Progress Indicator
children.description: Guides users through multi-step processes by providing a clear representation of progress.

children.name: Progress Ring
children.description: The progress ring visually displays progress in a circular form, showing the percentage of completion.

children.name: Radio Group
children.description: The radio group presents a set of options and allows only one option to be selected at a time.

children.name: Select
children.description: A select component provides a structured way for users to select from a menu. It enhances user experience by reducing input errors and improving efficiency when predefined options are appropriate.

children.name: Spinners
children.description: Spinners provide feedback when a system action is in progress. They should be used sparingly and in situations where users need confirmation that an action is taking place.

children.name: Tabs
children.description: Tabs organize related content into sections within a single page. This makes it easy for users to find what they need, without having to go to a different page. Users simply switch between the tabs.

children.name: Textarea
children.description: When users need space for detailed input, like comments or descriptions, use textarea fields. Textarea fields are built for multi-line text input and are perfect for detailed entries or notes. They al

children.name: Toast
children.description: Toasts provide immediate feedback to a user without disrupting their current task. The toast content is meant to be short and contextual, and provide the user with the information they need to know wi

children.name: Tooltip
children.description: Tooltips display brief, relevant information about an interactive element. They’re ideal for explaining unfamiliar icons, abbreviations, or functionality without occupying space in the primary layout.

children.name: Tree
children.description: The Tree component is ideal for presenting hierarchical data, such as file directories or organizational structures. It simplifies navigation and makes complex data sets more accessible.

children.name: Tree Grid
children.description: A Tree Grid is used to display complex hierarchical data where users need to navigate and interact with nested structures.

children.name: Border
children.description: Border utility classes are predefined CSS classes that provide quick and consistent styling for adding borders to elements.

children.name: Borders and Radius
children.description: Borders and radiuses are basic visual design elements that help create clarity, hierarchy, and a consistent look. In the Salesforce Lightning Design System (SLDS), borders and radiuses help create a c

children.description: Color establishes hierarchy, enhances branding, and fosters cohesion.

children.name: Box
children.description: Box utility classes help apply consistent borders and spacing for box styling to elements. These utilities simplify the process of visually defining containers and content areas within an application.

children.name: Canvas
children.description: The canvas is the main working area of a builder layout, and occupies the bulk of UX real estate. Here users can add and manipulate components, nodes and connectors, or data elements.

children.description: With click to create, users click (or use a keyboard) to open a contextual list of components that can be added to a selected position on the canvas.

children.description: The datetime picker is a form element that lets you select a date and time in one place.

children.name: Display Density
children.description: Density settings control global spacing, but different screen sizes require additional responsive adjustments.

children.description: Users can move elements on the screen by dragging and dropping them, using a mouse, keyboard, or other input device.

children.name: Nodes
children.description: Nodes are the building blocks of a workflow. Each one represents an action.

children.name: Padding
children.description: Padding is the space between a component's borders and its contents. Padding keeps content from getting crowded and helps maintain a clean, readable layout.

children.name: Panels
children.description: Panels present momentary tasks, properties, and supplemental information associated with the current builder, or a selection made within the canvas.

children.name: Print
children.description: The print utility class controls the visibility of components when a page is printed. This class can be used to hide elements that aren’t useful in a printed version of the interface.
